speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: chup-chap sab seh rahi hoon taaki dono gharo ki izzat bani rahe
text: Papa ne aaj bina mujhse pooche sasural walo ko bol diya ki main kabhi job nahi karungi, taaki mere past inter-caste relationship ka mudda phirse na uthe. Main is joint family aur saas ke taano ke beech apni identity kho chuki hoon, par unke faisle ka virodh nahi kar sakti. 'Log kya kahenge' aur bacchon ke future ke darr se main chup-chap sab seh rahi hoon taaki dono gharo ki izzat bani rahe.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirroring the relational conflict and the helplessness of being spoken for by family.
text: Tumhare papa ne tumhare bina pooche itna bada faisla le liya, aur ab is joint family ke dabaav mein tum chup-chap har cheez jhel rahi ho taaki sabke rishte salamat rahein.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, main virodh bhi nahi kar sakti. Is sasural mein saas ke taane toh roz ka kaam hai, upar se ab meri khud ki toh koi marzi ya pehchaan hi nahi bachi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirroring the duty framing where the Seeker hides her own needs to maintain the family role.
text: Apne aap ko itne taano ke beech bhi tumne poori tarah rok ke rakha hai, kyunki tumhe lagta hai ki is ghar ki bahu aur beti hone ka farz nibhana tumhari marzi se zyada zaroori hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: chup-chap sab seh rahi hoon taaki dono gharo ki izzat bani rahe
text: Farz ke naam pe hi toh meri poori zindagi qurban ho rahi hai. Apne bacchon ke future ka darr lagta hai, isiliye saas ki har kadwi baat sun leti hoon aur main chup-chap sab seh rahi hoon taaki dono gharo ki izzat bani rahe.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirroring the duration and weight of the burden she has been carrying.
text: Itne lambe samay se tum apne bacchon aur is parivaar ki shanti ke liye bina uff kiye lagataar itna bada bojh akele hi dho rahi ho.

speaker: Seeker
text: Akele hi hoon main. Sasural mein sab mujhe us purane inter-caste relationship ke liye gunehgaar mante hain. 'Log kya kahenge' ye soch soch ke papa ne mere sapne hi tod diye job karne ke.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirroring the feared consequence of society's judgment ruining her peace.
text: Tumhe yeh darr satata hai ki agar tumne apne haq ke liye aawaz uthayi, toh society phir se tumhari purani baatein kured kar tumhari izzat par sawal uthayegi.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: chup-chap sab seh rahi hoon taaki dono gharo ki izzat bani rahe
text: Bilkul wahi darr hai. Aur jab saas taane marti hai toh main chup-chap sab seh rahi hoon taaki dono gharo ki izzat bani rahe, par andar hi andar main toot chuki hoon. Kabhi kabhi toh lagta hai ghutan se saans ruk jayegi meri.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Gently exploring her somatic experience of suffocation to open up emotional depth.
text: Jab tumhe aisi ghutan hoti hai aur saans rukne jaisa mehsus hota hai, toh us akelepan mein tum khud ko kaise sambhalti ho?

speaker: Seeker
text: Bas ro leti hoon washroom mein jaakar. Bacchon ke samne rona bhi theek nahi lagta. Unhe kya jawaab doongi? Meri toh apni family ne bhi mera saath chhod diya biradari mein kya muh dikhayenge ke chakkar mein.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Validating her isolation and honoring the stigma cue softly without questioning.
text: Apno ne hi jab samajh ke chakkar mein munh pher liya, toh apne bacchon ki khaatir tumne apne aansu bhi washroom ke akelepan mein chupa liye hain...

speaker: Seeker
text: Sahi kahan aapne. Par sach kahoon, mujhe gussa aane laga hai ab apne papa par bhi. Kyun meri khushi kuch nahi hai? Kya mera haq nahi hai ki main job karun? Par phir lagta hai ki main bahut buri beti hoon jo aisa soch rahi hoon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reframing her near-anger into the deeper feeling of being trapped and helpless due to relational duties.
text: Lagta hai is waqt tum in tano aur faislon ke beech poori tarah se fasa hua aur helpless mehsoos kar rahi ho, jahan apne haq ki baat sochna bhi tumhe andar se ek apradhi jaisa feel karata hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, bilkul helpless. Bacchon ki dekh-bhal, saas ke taane, aur ye joint family ka dikhawa... sab mere akele ke kandhon par dal diya gaya hai. Aur job ka sapna toh waise hi khatam ho gaya.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Normalizing her exhaustion and separating her worth from the societal burden placed upon her.
text: Tumhari jagah koi bhi hota toh is dabaav mein aise hi thakan aur ghutan mehsoos karta, kyunki humare samaj mein aurton par dono gharo ki izzat ka bojh bina soche samjhe daal diya jata hai, aur isme tumhari kahin koi galti nahi hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Mujhe lagta tha ki main weak hoon. Par aapki baaton se thoda halka lag raha hai. Par job na karne ka dukh kam nahi ho raha. Main apne bachon ke liye independent banna chahti thi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Providing a brief systemic anecdote to show her struggle is a common structural issue.
text: Apne aas paas aur bhi aisi aurton se meri baat hoti hai; sasural mein joint family ke dabao mein khud ki pehchan ko bhool jana bahut common hai—sirf tumhara nahi, lekin koi bolega nahi kyunki samaj aur izzat ka darr sabko chup kara deta hai.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: chup-chap sab seh rahi hoon taaki dono gharo ki izzat bani rahe
text: Ye izzat shabd ne hum aurton ki jaan le rakhi hai. Saas din bhar mujhe us purane relationship ka taana marti hai. Main jawab dena chahti hoon, par main chup-chap sab seh rahi hoon taaki dono gharo ki izzat bani rahe.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reframing her urge to respond back into her overriding sense of duty.
text: Jab din bhar tumhare guzre hue kal ko lekar aisi baatein sunayi jati hain, tab bhi tum chup rehti ho kyunki tumhe andar se lagta hai ki in gharon ki neev bachana hi tumhara pehla farz hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Neev bachate bachate main poori jal chuki hoon andar se. Mujhe samajh nahi aa raha ki ab kya karoon, kaise main theek mehsus karoon bina kisi se lade?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Offering small, duty-compatible grounding options because she signaled readiness.
text: Aise mahaul mein thodi si saans lene ke liye hum do choti cheezein try kar sakte hain. Ek, jab taane bohot zyada lagne lagein, toh mentally ek 'stop' word soch kar wahan se kitchen ya kamre ke kaam ke bahane hat jana, ya do, din mein 10 minute bacchon ke pas hone par sirf apni dhyan unki baaton par lagana taaki dimaag thoda shant ho.

speaker: Seeker
text: Kitchen wala bahana toh main kabhi kabhi karti hoon, par us time dimaag wahi atka rehta hai ki unhone abhi kya taana mara. Bahut overthinking hoti hai. Batao kya karoon us waqt?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Walking through a single small actionable step step-by-step.
text: Pehle yeh karo ki jab dhyan atakne lage, toh kitchen ya washroom jao aur thande paani se cheentey apne chehre par maro... phir wapas aakar apni saans par do second dhyan do... bas itna hi aaj ke liye try karna, taaki overthinking ka chain toot sake.

speaker: Seeker
text: Thanda paani marna acha idea lag raha hai. Thodi cooling toh milegi is jalte hue dimaag ko. Par ye sasural ki taane-baazi kab khatam hogi?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Systemic reframing to show that taunts are about control, not her personal flaws.
text: Sahi kaha, dimaag ko thodi thandak milna zaroori hai. Joint family mein ye purani baaton ke taane aksar bas control banaye rakhne ka ek tarika hota hai, yeh tumhari kami nahi, balki unke apne khayalat aur insecurity ka nateeja hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Aap theek keh rahe ho. Unhe bas apna control chahiye, chahe papa hon ya meri saas. Unhe meri degree ya feelings se matlab nahi, unhe meri life control karni hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirroring her realization about the feared consequence being more about family control.
text: Mujhe aisa lag raha hai ki ab tum is baat ko saaf dekh pa rahi ho ki unke faisle tumhari kaabiliyat ki wajah se nahi, balki apni pakad aur dabdaba khone ke darr se jude hain.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, jab main apni degree dekhti hoon almari mein padi hui, toh lagta hai sab chhut gaya. Par ab itna pata hai ki usmein meri koi galti nahi hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Affirming her realization and validating her inherent worth regardless of her current situation.
text: Aur ye samajhna ek bahut bada kadam hai, kyunki tumhari degree aur tumhari padhai ki asli ahmiyat is ghar ke tano se tay nahi hoti; tumhari khud ki lagan aur samajhdaari us degree ki sachi pehchaan hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Shayad jab mere bache thode bade ho jayenge, toh main kuch na kuch apne liye chota-mota kaam ya tuition shuru kar lungi ghar se hi. Kabhi na kabhi koi raasta toh niklega.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirroring her shift in time perspective toward a future where she regains autonomy.
text: Tumhe aage ke samay mein umeed dikh rahi hai ki waqt thoda aage badhne par tum apne liye ek naya chota sa daira bana logi, jahan tum apni marzi aur pehchan phirse bunti hui dikhogi.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan, umeed pe hi toh duniya kayam hai. Agar main abhi toot gayi, toh mere bacchon ko kaun dekhega? Unke aane wale kal ke liye mujhe andar se theek rehna hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirroring her duty-based motivation to stay strong for her children.
text: Apne bacchon ka aane wala kal sanwarne ki yehi chahat tumhe is waqt andar se mazboot banati hai aur in sabke beech bhi har subah uthne ki himmat deti hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Sahi keh rahe hain aap. Thodi himmat toh mili hai aaj baatcheet karke. Pehle toh main bas andar hi andar ghut rahi thi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirroring the relief found in sharing the relational burden.
text: Jo bojh tumne kab se akele sambhala hua tha, use aaj baant lene par tumhare mann ki wo ghutan aur dabaav sach mein thoda kam mehsoos ho raha hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Jee, aapne bilkul sahi kaha. Aaj washroom wale aur saans lene wale tips pakka se try karungi jab dimaag kharab hoga. Kuch toh chota kadam apne liye uthana padega ab.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Praising her small step towards self-preservation within her constraints.
text: Apne liye waqt nikalne ka aur is dhyan batane wale tareeqe ka yeh chota sa iraada dekh kar sach mein bahut acha lag raha hai; tumhare paas apni bhalayi aur thode sakun ke liye yeh kadam uthane ka poora haq hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Shukriya aapka meri in sab baaton ko itne aaram se sunne ke liye. Aisa laga jaise kisi apne ne hi bina judge kiye meri ye pareshani samajh li ho.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflecting the connection built during the conversation.
text: Mujhe khushi hai ki yahan bina kisi rok-tok ke baatcheet karte hue tumhe ek sahara aur apna-pan mehsoos hua, yaad rakhna tum is mushkil safar mein bilkul akeli nahi ho.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ek aadarsh beti aur bahu hone ka bojh uthate huye main chup hoon taaki khandaan ki 'izzat' mitti mein na mile
text: Papa ne aaj achanak faisla suna diya ki meri choti behen ka love marriage nahi hoga aur uska rishta hamari hi caste mein tay kar diya hai. Saas ke kalesh ke beech meri apni shaadi shuda zindagi ek mazaak ban chuki hai, par main behen ke haq mein baap se bagawat nahi kar sakti. Ek aadarsh beti aur bahu hone ka bojh uthate huye main chup hoon taaki khandaan ki 'izzat' mitti mein na mile.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirroring the conflict between her love for her sister and the preservation of family relationships.
text: Tumhare papa ke is achanak faisle ne tumhe andar se hila diya hai, jahan ek taraf behen ka pyaar hai aur doosri taraf khandaan aur sasural ke rishte bachane ki majboori.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: main chup hoon taaki khandaan ki 'izzat' mitti mein na mile
text: Haan, behen subah se ro rahi hai aur uska dukh mujhe andar se khaa raha hai. Par meri khud ki halat aisi hai ki saas ke kalesh roz hote hain. Agar main abhi papa ke faisle ke khilaf gayi, toh pehle wo mujhe bolenge ki apna ghar toh sambhal nahi sakti. Isliye main chup hoon taaki khandaan ki 'izzat' mitti mein na mile.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirroring her fear that speaking up will invite direct attacks on her own fragile married life.
text: Yeh soch kar darr lagta hai ki agar behen ka saath dene ke liye aawaz uthayi, toh wahi saas aur maike wale tumhari hi zindagi aur khamiyon par ungli uthane lagenge.

speaker: Seeker
text: Bilkul wahi hoga. Seedha taana aayega ki tere sasural mein toh tera koi maan nahi. Main bas ghut rahi hoon dekh ke ki kaise meri behen ki zindagi ka bhi caste aur samaj ke naam pe sauda ho raha hai, aur main kuch nahi kar pa rahi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflecting the continuous weight of keeping everyone pleased over a long period.
text: Pichle kuch waqt se tum lagataar apne haq aur aawaz daba kar, roz in tano ke beech sirf sabki shanti banaye rakhne ki koshish kar rahi ho.

speaker: Seeker
text: Shanti toh kahin bhi nahi hai. Na yahan sasural mein aur na wahan maike mein. Mujhe sab se kat kar bas apne kamre mein akele baithna accha lagne laga hai aaj kal, lekin wo bhi naseeb kahan.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Exploring how she manages this isolation given the constant demands.
text: Jab sasural aur maike dono jagah aisi bandishein hain aur akele rehna bhi nahi milta, toh din bhar ke in kalesh aur behen ki fikar ko tum kaise sambhalti ho?

speaker: Seeker
text: Bathroom mein ro leti hoon chup ke se. Kisi aur ko toh bol bhi nahi sakti. Log sochein ki aisi kaun si aadarsh bahu hai jo har waqt kalesh karti hai ya rota muh leke ghumti hai, samaj wale toh mujhe hi gunehgaar bana denge.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Honoring the stigma cue softly by validating how unsafe it feels to show her pain.
text: Apne hi apno ke beech kisi se dukh baantna bhi ek jurm jaisa lagne laga hai tumhe, jahan samaj ke dosh ka darr aansuon ko akelepan mein hi bahne ko majboor kar deta hai...

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ek aadarsh beti aur bahu hone ka bojh uthate huye main chup hoon taaki khandaan ki 'izzat' mitti mein na mile
text: Ji, bilkul. Aur sabse zyada sharam tab aati hai jab behen umeed bhari nazaron se mujhe dekhti hai aur main nazar chura leti hoon. Ek aadarsh beti aur bahu hone ka bojh uthate huye main chup hoon taaki khandaan ki 'izzat' mitti mein na mile, par main ek bahut buri behen hoon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reframing her self-blame back into the heavy burden of duty she is carrying.
text: Aadarsh bahu aur beti ke farz ne tumhe aise baandh liya hai ki us role ko nibhane ki keemat tumhe apne hi nazaron mein ek choti behen ka gunehgaar bana rahi hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan... Aaj behen ro rahi thi phone par ki didi papa ko kuch toh bolo. Aur us waqt mujhe meri saas ke shaam ke tiffin ki zyada fikar thi, taaki naya kalesh na ho. Kaisi aurat ban gayi hoon main?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Naming the core conflict of being trapped and helpless due to relational duties.
text: Lagta hai is aadarsh aurat banne ki hod mein tum khud ko poori tarah fasa hua aur helpless mehsoos kar rahi ho, jahan behen ka dard mehsoos karne par bhi apna haq ya stand na le paana tumhe kha raha hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Fasi hui hi toh hoon. Meri himmat hi nahi bachi ab. Bagawat karna mere bas ka nahi kyunki mujhe pata hai result kya hoga. Bas zindagi kat rahi hai lachari mein.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Normalizing her exhaustion and lachari in the context of intense societal expectations.
text: Tumhari halat mein aisi lachari aur thakan mehsoos karna bilkul swabhavik hai; humare samaaj mein aurton se umeed ki jati hai ki wo apna wajood bhul kar sabko jod kar rakhein, aur is dabaav ke aage toot jana tumhari kamzori nahi hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Shayad aap sahi keh rahe hain. Maine hamesha sabko jodne ki koshish ki, aur badle mein meri pehchan hi mitti mein mil gayi. Main kabhi apna bhala soch hi nahi payi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Brief systemic anecdote showing that this loss of identity is a common structural issue for women in this region.
text: Maike aur sasural mein aadarsh aurat banne ke chakkar mein aese apni pehchan bhool jana bohot common hai—sirf tumhara hi dard nahi hai ye, lekin koi aurat khul kar bolegi nahi kyunki izzat aur samaj ka taana sabko dara deta hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Bas wahi taane toh din raat sunne ko milte hain. Kabhi kabhi toh mujhe papa par itna krodh aata hai ki main jaa ke unhe khari-khoti suna doon, par main kuch nahi kar paati.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reframing her near-anger into the overriding sense of family duty and impossibility.
text: Andar hi andar papa ke faisle par tumhe shadeed krodh ubalta hua mehsoos hota hai, phir bhi un rishton ki maryada aur ghar ki shanti ka khayal tumhare us gusse ko bahar aane hi nahi deta.

speaker: Seeker
text: Nahi aata bahar, aana hota toh kabka aa chuka hota. In maryadaon ne mera dam ghont diya hai. Mujhe samajh nahi aa raha ki is guilt ke saath ab main aage kya karoon?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Offering small, duty-compatible grounding options as she explicitly asked what to do.
text: Is bhaari manasthiti mein, apne guilt aur thakan ko thoda sambhalne ke liye hum do chote kadam soch sakte hain. Ek, behen ko chupke se ek chota sa text bhej dena jisme sirf tumhara sath jhalakta ho bina papa ka zikar kiye, ya do, jab kalesh zyada ho toh kitchen ke kaam ke waqt apni saans par do minute theher kar dhyan lagana.

speaker: Seeker
text: Saans wala toh shayad bhool jaungi tension mein. Par behen ko text karne wala main try kar sakti hoon. Par message likhun kya ki usko bura bhi na lage aur main fasu bhi na?

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Breaking down the suggestion into step-by-step actions for sending the message safely.
text: Sabse pehle behen ko shaam ke us akele waqt mein msg karna jab saas tiffin mein busy hon, likhna ki 'main chup zaroor hoon par main tere dukh mein tere saath khadi hoon'... aur uske baad us msg ko delete kar dena... bas itna hi aaj ke liye try karna.

speaker: Seeker
text: Delete karne ka idea theek hai, safe rahega. Kam se kam usse ye toh pata chalega ki main usko akela nahi chhodna chahti, chahe saas-sasural ki wajah se majboor hi kyun na hoon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirroring her relief at finding a way to connect with her sister safely.
text: Behen ko bas itna jatana ki tum is majboori mein bhi uske dard se judi ho, us khamoshi ke bojh ko tum dono ke beech thoda toh halka zaroor kar dega.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan thoda toh halka hoga. Mujhe waise waqai lagta hai ki pata nahi ye log aurton ko bolne ka haq kab denge? Meri behen apni marzi ka pati tak nahi chun sakti.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Providing a systemic reframe on how 'izzat' is structurally used to silence women, validating her thought.
text: Tumhari ye sochna bilkul theek hai; humare yahan 'khandaan ki izzat' ko hamesha aurton ki aawaz dabane aur caste ki boundary banaye rakhne ke tool ki tarah use kiya jata hai, isme kasoor tumhara ya behen ka bilkul nahi hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Bilkul... humein sirf rules follow karne ka machine samjha jata hai. Meri saas ke kalesh bhi toh waise hi shuru hote hain agar main apna dimag laga loon ghar ke kisi kaam mein.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflecting her awareness of how the relational dynamics punish any expression of individuality.
text: Jab charo taraf se dabaav sirf tumhare sir aata hai, toh tum saaf dekh pa rahi ho ki yahan khud ka wajood dikhana bhi kisi gunaah ki tarah mana jata hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Gunaah hi hai. Lekin main ab is guilt mein nahi jiyungi ki behen ke saath gaddari ki. Main chupke se hi sahi, apni taraf se uska sahara banungi. Aaj shaam ko main usko msg pakka karungi.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Mirroring her determination to act within the bounds of her current reality.
text: Shaam ke us akele waqt ko istemaal karke behen se judna sach mein bohot bada aur himmat bhara chota sa kadam dikh raha hai aage ke liye.

speaker: Seeker
coping_shade_signal: Ek aadarsh beti aur bahu hone ka bojh uthate huye main chup hoon taaki khandaan ki 'izzat' mitti mein na mile
text: Himmat karni toh padegi apni tarike se. Beshak, ek aadarsh beti aur bahu hone ka bojh uthate huye main chup hoon taaki khandaan ki 'izzat' mitti mein na mile, par main ek acchi behen banne ki koshish toh kar hi sakti hoon.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Validating her subtle form of resistance and preserving her worth.
text: Apna dukh aur bojh sambhalne ke bawajood apni behen ke lagav ko zinda rakhna asal mein sachhe pyaar aur mazbooti ki pehchaan hai, jo kisi 'izzat' ke taane se mitti nahi ho sakti.

speaker: Seeker
text: Bohot dino baad kisi se dil ki baat aise boli hai. Pehle lag raha tha jaise bas main hi galat hoon aur darr-pok hoon, par aapse baat karke thoda halka mehsoos ho raha hai.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflecting on how facing the feared consequence of sharing her pain actually brought relief.
text: Dimaag ka is tarah bhaari hona laazmi tha jab saara dosh apne sir par nazar aa raha tha, par ab us guilt ka thoda utar jana sach mein sukoon dene wala hai.

speaker: Seeker
text: Ji, sahi keh rahe hain. Ab uth ke mujhe wapas kitchen ki taiyari dekhni padegi warna shaam ka alag natak chalu ho jayega ghar mein.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Acknowledging her return to her ongoing duties.
text: Chup reh kar bhi apne farz ko sambhalne ke liye tum apna agla kadam utha rahi ho, kitchen ki is taiyari mein wapas jaate hue.

speaker: Seeker
text: Haan ji. Aaj shaam ka behen ko msg bhejna mujhe meri himmat yaad dilayega. Meri aawaz sunne ka bohot bohot shukriya.

speaker: Supporter
strategy_intent: Reflecting the shared connection and ending softly.
text: Aapki bhalayi aur in mushkil rishton ke beech se raasta nikalne ki ye himmat dekh kar mujhe bhi yahan baith kar sukoon mehsoos ho raha hai, kabhi zaroorat ho toh yaad rakhna koi yahan sunne wala zarur maujood hai.
